info.name: text-delete
info.text: A good theory needs to be built from the ground up.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Theories take time and planning to become permant fixtures in society.

info.name: text-insert
info.text: This theory doesn't have a strong foundation.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: A theory doesn't always have a strong foundation.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: In order to persist, a theory must have a strong foundation.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Theories should be built out of brick and mortor, not straw and mud.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: A lasting theory should be built out of the strongest materials available.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Theories can deteriorate and fall down after time has passed.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Theories are built and stay in place on solid ground.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: A theory starts out with a blueprint

info.name: text-delete
info.text: A paper-thin theory will burn down in a fire.
